revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蕪木古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蕪木古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蕪木古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蕪木古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Sanmu|qid=Q873528|1=Sanmu}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.641667|N|140.451667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
{{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Kaburaki Kofun Cluster | image = | Location = Kaburaki, Matsuo Town, Sammu City, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|30|N|140|27|6|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli | Size = Total length: 76m, 47m | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end | Excavated Artifacts = Sue pottery, iron straight swords, bronze horse fittings, gilt-bronze drawstring-shaped container, gilt-bronze sheath for a kozuka, etc. (Kofun No. 5) | Burial Facility = Horizontal stone chamber (Kofun No. 5) | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Kaburaki Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=30|lon_deg=140|lon_min=27|lon_sec=6|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|38|30|N|140|27|6|E}}}} }}
'''Kaburaki Kofun Cluster''' ({{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburaki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in Kaburaki, Matsuo Town, Sammu City, Chiba Prefecture.
== Overview == The Kaburaki Kofun Cluster is located on a plateau at an altitude of 40 meters, marking the eastern edge of the {{ill|Bōsō Hill Range|ja|房総台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Hill Range|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 房総丘陵|1=Bōsō Hill Range|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5005697}} in Kaburaki, Matsuo Town, Sammu City. The cluster consists of 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}) and 15 [[Round Kofun|round tumuli]] ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Excavations were conducted on two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun Nos. 1 and 5) by Nihon University in 1952 and 1953. Subsequent investigations of Kofun No. 5 were carried out by the Sammu Archaeological Institute in 1989, involving mound surveys and reexaminations of the horizontal stone chamber. Kofun Nos. 17 and 18 were excavated in 1984 during a sand extraction project but were subsequently destroyed.
Kofun No. 1, known as {{ill|Asahino-Oka Kofun|ja|朝日ノ岡古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahino-Oka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is located within the precincts of {{ill|Kaburaki Hachiman Shrine|ja|蕪木八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The mound is partially eroded on the south side due to shrine construction. It measures 76 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 48 meters and a frontal width of 40 meters. The double moats surrounding the mound were disrupted during the construction of Chiba Prefectural Route 62. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, and tubular beads have been recorded. The mound featured rows of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, including cylindrical, human, horse-shaped, and bird-shaped figures.
Kofun No. 5 is located approximately 150 meters southeast of Kofun No. 1. This smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus measures 47 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 20 meters and a frontal width of 20 meters. Despite its modest size, it yielded an exceptional array of burial goods, including 12 iron straight swords, bronze horse fittings, a gilt-bronze sheath for a {{ill|Kozuka|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuka|qq=}}, and a gilt-bronze drawstring-shaped container. The horizontal stone chamber features a two-room structure, and though partially collapsed, it remains notable for its construction and contents.
The construction period is estimated to span the late 6th century, with Kofun No. 1 attributed to the latter half of the century and Kofun No. 5 to the very end.
== Location == The Kaburaki Kofun Cluster is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba)|qq=}}, central to the {{ill|Kujukuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Plain|qq=}}, approximately 1.5 kilometers north of {{ill|Matsuo Station (Chiba)|en|Matsuo Station (Chiba)|qid=Q5365257|lt=Matsuo Station (Chiba)|1=Matsuo Station (Chiba)}} on the JR East {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|qid=Q1191364|lt=Sobu Main Line|1=Sōbu Main Line}}. It is adjacent to {{ill|Matsuo Castle (Kazusa)|ja|松尾城 (上総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Castle (Kazusa)|1=Q11529595|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11529595}}, built by Ōta Sukeyoshi after the abolition of domains and establishment of prefectures. Nearby are the {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Dazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|大塚古墳群 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山室姫塚古墳}} to the north and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream.
== Access == * 20-minute walk from JR East's Matsuo Station on the Sobu Main Line. * From Narita International Airport's Terminal 2, take the Airport Shuttle Bus to "Matsuodai Industrial Park Entrance."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names'' (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12). Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|en|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|qid=Q11614636|1=Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|lt=Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|qid=Q11614636|lt=Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum}} * {{ill|500 Attractions of Boso|ja|房総の魅力500選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/500 Attractions of Boso|1=Best 500 attractions in Chiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11496691}}
== External Links == * [https://sammukanko.jp/attraction/239/ Kaburaki Kofun Cluster] - Sammu City Tourism Association * [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001557.html Asahino-Oka Kofun (Kaburaki No. 1)] - Sammu City Board of Education * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-5.html Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum - Exhibition Introduction: Asahino-Oka Kofun]
* [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] ...l|Ōzutsumi Gongenzuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kaburagi Kofun Group|lt=Kabuki Kofun Cluster|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabuki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} downstream, which predominantly feature {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun ... 5 KB (641 words) - 21:13, 15 January
{{Kofun navbar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped Kofun ... 5 KB (641 words) - 21:13, 15 January
